title: ONGC Geophysics — Full-length Mock 01

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Mohorovičić discontinuity separates:
questions.solution: The Moho is the seismic boundary where P-wave velocity increases abruptly from ~6.5–7.2 km/s in the lower crust to ~8.1 km/s in the upper mantle, marking the crust–mantle interface.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which seismic wave is the fastest and travels through both solids and liquids?
questions.solution: P-waves (primary/compressional) are body waves with the highest velocity. They propagate by compressional deformation and can travel through solids, liquids, and gases.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which type of plate boundary is associated with the formation of new oceanic lithosphere?
questions.solution: At divergent boundaries (mid-ocean ridges), magma rises from the asthenosphere, creating new oceanic crust through seafloor spreading.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Earth's main magnetic field is primarily generated by:
options: Convection currents in the outer core
Solar wind interaction with the magnetosphere
questions.solution: The geodynamo theory explains that the main field is generated by convective motions of the electrically conducting liquid iron in the outer core.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The standard value of gravity at the Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.solution: Standard gravity is 9.80665 m/s² = 980.665 Gal = 981 mGal (rounded). Note: 1 Gal = 1 cm/s² = 100 mGal.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The half-life of Uranium-238 used in geological dating is approximately:
questions.solution: U-238 decays to Pb-206 with a half-life of 4.468 billion years (~4.5 Ga), making it suitable for dating rocks older than ~10 million years.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: S-waves are also known as _____ waves because they produce side-to-side motion.
questions.solution: S-waves (Secondary/Shear waves) involve particle motion perpendicular to the propagation direction. They cannot travel through fluids.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Richter magnitude scale measures:
options: The intensity of shaking at a specific location
The total energy released by an earthquake
The depth of the earthquake focus
The surface area of the fault rupture
questions.solution: Magnitude (ML, Mw, etc.) quantifies the energy released at the source. Intensity (MMI) measures the observed effects at a given location.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A focal mechanism solution (beach ball diagram) is derived from:
options: First-motion polarity readings at multiple stations
questions.solution: First-motion focal mechanisms are constructed by plotting the polarity (up/down) of P-wave first arrivals on a focal sphere and fitting two nodal planes.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Wadati-Benioff zone is associated with:
options: Subduction zone seismicity along inclined planes
questions.solution: Wadati-Benioff zones are inclined planes of intermediate to deep earthquakes (70–700 km) that trace the descending slab in subduction zones.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A LaCoste-Romberg gravimeter uses which principle to measure gravity variations?
questions.solution: The LaCoste-Romberg gravimeter employs a zero-length spring system where the spring force is proportional to the total length, enabling extremely sensitive gravity measurements.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-air correction accounts for:
questions.solution: Free-air correction (0.3086 mGal/m) adjusts the gravity reading for the elevation difference between the station and the reference datum, without accounting for the mass between them.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A positive Bouguer anomaly over a sedimentary basin typically indicates:
options: Dense basement rock at shallow depth
High heat flow in the crust
questions.solution: Positive Bouguer anomalies reflect excess mass. Over basins, a positive anomaly suggests dense basement rocks at shallow depth (i.e., a thin sedimentary section).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The depth to the center of a spherical anomalous body can be estimated from a gravity profile using:
questions.solution: All three methods can estimate depth: the half-width rule gives quick estimates, Euler deconvolution provides structural indices, and forward modeling fits the entire anomaly shape.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnetic declination at a location is the angle between:
questions.solution: Magnetic declination (D) is the horizontal angle between true (geographic) north and magnetic north, measured positive eastward.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Wenner electrode configuration, the spacing between adjacent electrodes is:
questions.solution: The Wenner array uses equal spacing 'a' between all four electrodes (A-M-N-B equally spaced), making field setup simple but requiring more power.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The static shift problem in magnetotelluric (MT) data is primarily caused by:
questions.solution: Static shift is a frequency-independent shift of MT apparent resistivity curves caused by small-scale, near-surface resistivity heterogeneities that distort the electric field.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Induced polarization (IP) measurements are most sensitive to:
questions.solution: IP effects arise from electrode polarization (metallic minerals) and membrane polarization (clay particles), making IP a key tool for mineral exploration and clay detection.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Self-potential (SP) anomalies over sulfide ore bodies are typically:
questions.solution: SP anomalies over massive sulfide bodies are characteristically negative (up to several hundred mV) due to electrochemical (oxidation-reduction) processes at the ore–wallrock interface.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In magnetotelluric (MT) soundings, the skin depth δ is proportional to:
questions.solution: Skin depth δ ≈ 503√(ρ/f) metres, where ρ is resistivity (Ω·m) and f is frequency (Hz). Lower frequencies penetrate deeper, and higher resistivity increases penetration.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Snell's law relates the angles of incidence and refraction to:
questions.solution: Snell's law: sin θ₁/V₁ = sin θ₂/V₂. The refraction angle depends on the ratio of velocities across the interface, not density or impedance alone.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In seismic refraction surveys, the critical distance is the minimum offset at which:
options: The first arrival is a reflected wave
The refracted wave emerges as the first arrival
The direct wave and refracted wave arrive simultaneously
The head wave is no longer generated
questions.solution: At the crossover distance (critical distance), the travel times of the direct wave and the critically refracted wave are equal. Beyond this, the refracted wave arrives first.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The normal moveout (NMO) correction removes the effect of:
options: Source-receiver offset on reflection travel times
questions.solution: NMO correction adjusts reflection travel times to the zero-offset (normal incidence) time, removing the hyperbolic increase in travel time with offset for a flat reflector.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The common midpoint (CMP) method improves signal-to-noise ratio by:
options: Stacking traces that share the same subsurface reflection point
questions.solution: CMP stacking sums traces with the same midpoint, enhancing coherent reflections (signal) while canceling random noise, improving S/N by √n for n-fold coverage.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic migration is necessary because:
options: Raw stacked sections correctly position dipping reflectors
Unmigrated sections place reflectors at their true subsurface positions
Unmigrated sections misposition dipping events and collapse diffractions
questions.solution: Migration moves dipping reflectors to their true positions (up-dip), collapses diffractions from point sources, and focuses energy. Unmigrated sections show events at incorrect positions.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In land seismic surveys, the most commonly used seismic source is:
questions.solution: Vibroseis ( hydraulic vibrator) is the dominant land source — it sweeps through a frequency range over several seconds, is repeatable, environmentally friendly, and safe.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The spontaneous potential (SP) log measures:
options: Natural electrical potential between borehole and surface
questions.solution: SP logs record the natural DC potential difference between an electrode in the borehole and a surface reference, used to identify permeable beds and estimate formation water resistivity.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The invasion process in a permeable formation drilled with mud causes:
options: Resistivity profile: Rxo → Ri → Rt (increasing outward)
Resistivity profile: Rt → Ri → Rxo (decreasing outward)
questions.solution: Mud filtrate invades the formation, creating: flushed zone (Rxo, mud filtrate saturated), transition zone (Ri), and uninvaded zone (Rt, native formation). Since Rw < Rmf typically, Rxo > Rt.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The density log determines porosity by measuring:
options: Gamma ray absorption by formation electrons
questions.solution: The density tool emits gamma rays and detects backscattered radiation. Higher electron density (bulk density) means more absorption, allowing porosity calculation: ρb = φρf + (1−φ)ρma.

figure.caption: Standard well log display: GR, SP, resistivity (LLD/LLS), density-neutron, and sonic tracks

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Wyllie time-average equation relates sonic travel time to porosity as:
questions.solution: Wyllie equation: φ = (Δtobs − Δtma)/(Δtf − Δtma), where Δtobs is measured travel time, Δtma is matrix travel time, and Δtf is fluid travel time (≈189 μs/ft for water).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The gamma ray log is primarily used to differentiate between:
questions.solution: Gamma ray logs measure natural radioactivity (K, U, Th). Shales have high GR due to potassium-rich clay minerals; sands/carbonates have low GR. This defines the shale volume.

figure.caption: Standard well log display: GR, SP, resistivity (LLD/LLS), density-neutron, and sonic tracks

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The neutron log responds primarily to:
questions.solution: Neutron tools emit fast neutrons that lose energy through collisions with hydrogen atoms. The thermal neutron count rate is inversely related to hydrogen index, which approximates porosity in liquid-filled pores.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Nyquist frequency in digital seismic recording is:
options: The highest frequency in the signal
questions.solution: Nyquist frequency = 1/(2Δt), where Δt is the sampling interval. Frequencies above Nyquist cause aliasing. For Δt = 2 ms, Nyquist = 250 Hz.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A bandpass filter in seismic processing is used to:
options: Remove all frequencies below and above a specified passband
Shift the phase of all frequencies
Convert time domain to depth domain
questions.solution: Bandpass filtering removes unwanted noise outside the signal bandwidth. For example, a 10–60 Hz passband removes low-frequency ground roll and high-frequency ambient noise.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The seismic trace can be modeled as a convolution of:
questions.solution: The convolutional model: trace = wavelet * reflectivity + noise. The reflectivity series represents subsurface impedance contrasts, and the wavelet is the source signature.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The primary goal of deconvolution in seismic processing is to:
options: Compress the wavelet to improve temporal resolution
questions.solution: Deconvolution shortens (compresses) the seismic wavelet, effectively removing the source signature effects and improving the resolution of closely spaced reflectors.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In AVO (Amplitude Versus Offset) analysis, a Class III AVO anomaly is characterized by:
options: Negative intercept and increasing negative amplitude with offset
Positive intercept and decreasing amplitude with offset
Positive intercept and increasing amplitude with offset
questions.solution: Class III AVO (Rutherford & Williams): negative AVO intercept (R₀) with negative gradient (G), typical of low-impedance gas sands. The amplitude becomes more negative (brighter) with offset.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Water saturation (Sw) in a formation can be estimated using:
questions.solution: Archie's equation: Sw = (aRw/φ^m Rt)^(1/n), where Rw = formation water resistivity, Rt = true resistivity, φ = porosity, a = tortuosity, m = cementation exponent, n = saturation exponent.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: According to the Airy model of isostasy, mountains are compensated by:
options: Lateral density variations in the crust
Root zones where thick crust extends into the denser mantle
Thickening of the lithospheric mantle only
questions.solution: Airy isostasy: topographic highs have deep crustal roots (like icebergs). Higher mountains have deeper roots, with the crust–mantle boundary (Moho) depressed beneath them.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The peak ground acceleration (PGA) in a seismic hazard analysis is most directly related to:
options: Distance from the source and local site conditions
questions.solution: PGA depends on earthquake magnitude, source-to-site distance, and local site effects (soil amplification, topography). Attenuation relations (GMPEs) model PGA as a function of M, R, and site class.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A student scored 72% in an exam where the passing mark is 60%. If the total marks are 200, by how many marks did the student exceed the passing mark?
questions.solution: Student scored: 72% of 200 = 144. Passing: 60% of 200 = 120. Excess = 144 − 120 = 24 marks.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A can complete a task in 12 days and B can complete it in 18 days. Working together, how many days will they take to finish the work?
questions.solution: A's rate = 1/12 per day; B's rate = 1/18 per day. Combined = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36 per day. Days = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A shopkeeper buys an article for ₹800 and sells it at a 25% profit. What is the selling price?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of five numbers is 42. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 38. What is the excluded number?
questions.solution: Total of 5 numbers = 5 × 42 = 210. Total of 4 numbers = 4 × 38 = 152. Excluded number = 210 − 152 = 58.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two dice are thrown simultaneously. What is the probability that the sum of the numbers is 7?
questions.solution: Total outcomes = 36. Favorable pairs for sum 7: (1,6),(2,5),(3,4),(4,3),(5,2),(6,1) = 6 outcomes. Probability = 6/36 = 1/6.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: What comes next in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.solution: Differences: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Next term = 30 + 12 = 42. Alternatively: n(n+1) → 6×7 = 42.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If in a code language, COMPUTER is written as RFUVQNPC, how is MEDICINE written?
questions.solution: Pattern: Each letter is replaced by the letter symmetrically opposite in the alphabet (A↔Z, B↔Y, etc.). M→O, E→J, D→J, I→R, C→X, I→R, N→M, E→J. Wait, let me recheck. C→O, O→L, M→N, P→K, U→F, T→G, E→V, R→I. The pattern shifts each letter by a fixed offset. MEDICINE → EOJDJEFM.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:2. If there are 40 students total, how many girls are there?
questions.solution: Boys = 3x, Girls = 2x. Total = 5x = 40, so x = 8. Girls = 2 × 8 = 16.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A car travels 150 km in 3 hours and then 200 km in 4 hours. What is its average speed for the entire journey?
questions.solution: Total distance = 150 + 200 = 350 km. Total time = 3 + 4 = 7 hours. Average speed = 350/7 = 50 km/h.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Statements: All scientists are researchers. Some researchers are teachers. Conclusions: (I) Some scientists are teachers. (II) Some teachers are scientists.
questions.solution: From the Venn diagram: 'Some researchers are teachers' doesn't necessarily overlap with 'scientists'. The teachers could be among researchers who are not scientists. Neither conclusion is valid.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A company's revenue (in ₹ lakhs) for five years: 2018: 120, 2019: 150, 2020: 135, 2021: 180, 2022: 210. What is the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) from 2018 to 2022?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: ₹10,000 is invested at 8% per annum simple interest for 3 years. What is the total amount received?

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Ramesh starts from point A, walks 5 km north, turns right and walks 3 km, turns right again and walks 5 km. How far is he from point A?
questions.solution: After walking 5 km N → 3 km E → 5 km S, he is 3 km east of A. North and south cancel out. Distance = 3 km.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In how many ways can 5 students be seated in a row such that two particular students always sit together?
questions.solution: Treat the 2 students as one unit → 4 units to arrange = 4! = 24. The 2 students can swap within their unit = 2! = 2. Total = 24 × 2 = 48.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A can fill a tank in 20 hours and Pipe B can empty it in 30 hours. If both are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.solution: A's rate = 1/20 per hour (filling), B's rate = 1/30 per hour (emptying). Net rate = 1/20 − 1/30 = 1/60 per hour. Time = 60 hours.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a photograph, Ravi said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the person in the photograph related to Ravi?
questions.solution: Grandfather's only son = Ravi's father. Father's daughter = Ravi's sister (or Ravi himself if female, but the pronoun 'she' indicates sister).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle is 72 cm² and its length is 12 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.solution: Breadth = Area/Length = 72/12 = 6 cm. Perimeter = 2(L + B) = 2(12 + 6) = 36 cm.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The LCM of two numbers is 180 and their HCF is 6. If one number is 36, what is the other number?
questions.solution: LCM × HCF = Product of numbers. 180 × 6 = 36 × x. x = 1080/36 = 30.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a group of 100 people, 70 speak Hindi, 60 speak English, and 30 speak both. How many speak neither language?
questions.solution: Using inclusion-exclusion: Hindi ∪ English = 70 + 60 − 30 = 100. Since total = 100, neither = 100 − 100 = 0.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat travels 24 km upstream in 6 hours and 24 km downstream in 3 hours. What is the speed of the current?
questions.solution: Upstream speed = 24/6 = 4 km/h. Downstream speed = 24/3 = 8 km/h. Speed of current = (8 − 4)/2 = 2 km/h.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If sin θ = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is cos θ?
questions.solution: Using sin²θ + cos²θ = 1: cos²θ = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25. cos θ = 4/5 (positive in Q1).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
questions.solution: A doctor works in a hospital; a teacher works in a school. The relationship is professional : workplace.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If January 1, 2024 was a Monday, what day of the week was January 1, 2025?
questions.solution: 2024 is a leap year (366 days). 366 mod 7 = 2. Monday + 2 = Wednesday.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a 60-litre mixture, the ratio of milk to water is 3:1. How much water must be added to make the ratio 3:2?
questions.solution: Milk = 45 litres, Water = 15 litres. For ratio 3:2, milk stays 45, so water = 45 × 2/3 = 30 litres. Additional water = 30 − 15 = 15 litres.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Which number is the odd one out: 3, 5, 7, 12, 17, 21?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 7, 17 are all prime numbers. 12 and 21 are not. Among these, 12 is the only even composite number, making it the odd one out in this set.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which organization is the nodal agency for petroleum and natural gas in India?
options: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
questions.solution: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) is the government ministry responsible for oil and gas policy, regulation, and administration in India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC was founded in which year?
questions.solution: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC) was established on August 14, 1956, as a government company. It was later incorporated as a public limited company in 1994.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bombay High offshore oil field is located in which basin?
questions.solution: Bombay High (now Mumbai High) is India's largest offshore oil field, located about 160 km west of Mumbai in the Mumbai Offshore Basin, discovered in 1974.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian satellite series is primarily used for communication and broadcasting?
questions.solution: Both INSAT (Indian National Satellite) and GSAT (Geostationary Satellite) series are used for communication, broadcasting, and meteorological observations over India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Paris Agreement on climate change aims to limit global warming to:
questions.solution: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit warming to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels, with efforts to limit it to 1.5°C.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian city is known as the 'Oil Capital of India'?
questions.solution: Mumbai is often called the Oil Capital of India due to the presence of major oil companies (ONGC, BPCL, HPCL, Reliance) and the Mumbai High offshore oil field.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is commonly used in LPG cylinders for domestic cooking in India?
questions.solution: LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas) used in Indian households is primarily a mixture of propane (C₃H₈) and butane (C₄H₁₀).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first offshore oil well was drilled at:
questions.solution: India's first offshore well was drilled at Aliabet in the Gulf of Cambay (Khambhat) in 1962, before the discovery of Bombay High.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Krishna-Godavari Basin is located off the coast of which Indian state?
questions.solution: The Krishna-Godavari (KG) Basin is a prolific hydrocarbon-producing basin located along the eastern coast of India, primarily off Andhra Pradesh and Odisha.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which of the following is India's largest oil producer by volume?
questions.solution: ONGC (through its subsidiary ONGC Videsh and domestic operations) is India's largest crude oil producer, accounting for roughly 60-70% of India's domestic production.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most similar in meaning to 'ABUNDANT':
questions.solution: Abundant means existing in large quantities; plentiful. It is the antonym of scarce or limited.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most opposite in meaning to 'BENIGN':
questions.solution: Benign means gentle, kindly, or not harmful. Malignant means harmful, hostile, or cancerous — the direct opposite.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Identify the grammatically correct sentence:
options: Neither the teacher nor the students was present.
Each of the boys have completed their assignment.
The committee has decided to approve the proposal.
She don't know the answer to the question.
questions.solution: 'The committee has decided' is correct — 'committee' is a collective noun taking a singular verb. Other options: (A) should use 'were', (B) should use 'has', (D) should use 'doesn't'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does the idiom 'to burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night
questions.solution: 'Burn the midnight oil' means to work or study late into the night, originally referring to working by oil lamp light before electricity.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read the passage: 'The monsoon is a seasonal wind pattern that brings heavy rainfall to the Indian subcontinent. It is driven by the differential heating of land and sea.' What primarily drives the monsoon?
options: Differential heating of land and sea
questions.solution: The passage explicitly states the monsoon is driven by the differential heating of land and sea, which creates pressure gradients that reverse wind direction seasonally.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: A person who loves books is called a:
questions.solution: Bibliophile = book lover (biblio = book, philia = love). Philatelist collects stamps; Numismatist collects coins; Misanthropist dislikes humanity.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'He is proficient ___ English and French.'
questions.solution: 'Proficient in' is the correct collocation. We say 'proficient in a language' or 'proficient at a skill'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Rearrange to form a meaningful sentence: (P) is (Q) the (R) conservation (S) of (T) water (U) important (V) an
questions.solution: Correct order: 'An (V) the (Q) conservation (R) of (S) water (T) is (P) important (U)' → 'The conservation of water is an important (matter).'

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most opposite in meaning to 'TRANSPARENT':
questions.solution: Transparent means allowing light to pass through clearly. Opaque means not allowing light to pass through — the direct opposite.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'By the time we reach the station, the train __________.'
questions.solution: Future perfect tense is used for an action that will be completed before another future action. 'Will have left' indicates the train's departure will precede our arrival.
